event: acute New York Heart Association Class IV dyspnoea
multiple hospital attendances in the preceding 8 weeks
symptoms attributed to recurrent viral pericarditis
decreased breath sounds at bilateral lung bases
organized echogenic material adjacent to the right ventricle
significant tricuspid and mitral inflow variation (62% and 27%)
dilated and fixed inferior vena cava
repeat TTE demonstrated small residual effusion (1.3 cm)
akinetic and adherent right ventricular free wall
gram-positive bacillus in pericardial fluid culture
repeat TTE demonstrated increasing pericardial effusion (1.8 cm)
cardiac magnetic resonance imaging confirmed 2.6 cm pericardial effusion
marked thickening and fibrosis of the pericardium
mild reduction of LV and RV end-diastolic volumes
multidisciplinary meeting involving Cardiology, Cardiothoracic Surgery, and Infectious Diseases
clinical deterioration with significant exertional dyspnoea
pericardium resected over the right ventricle
post-operative TTE demonstrated minimal pericardial effusion
